name: Mechatronics Engineering
description: Mechatronics Engineering integrates mechanical, electronic, and computer engineering to design intelligent systems and automated processes. The curriculum covers sensors, actuators, control systems, robotics, and industrial automation.

subjects.name: Mechatronic Systems
subjects.description: Introduction to mechatronic system design integrating mechanical, electronic, and software components. Covers system modeling, sensors, actuators, and microcontroller-based control.
learning_outcomes: Understand the interdisciplinary nature of mechatronic systems
Model basic mechatronic systems using block diagrams
Select appropriate sensors and actuators for applications
Design simple microcontroller-based mechatronic systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Mechatronic systems are the foundation of modern automated manufacturing and smart product design.

subjects.name: Sensors & Actuators
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of industrial sensors and actuators including temperature, pressure, proximity, and optical sensors along with electric, hydraulic, and pneumatic actuators. Covers signal conditioning and interfacing.
learning_outcomes: Classify and select sensors for various industrial applications
Interface sensors with microcontrollers and data acquisition systems
Analyze actuator types including electric, hydraulic, and pneumatic
Design signal conditioning circuits for sensor outputs
subjects.industry_relevance: Sensors and actuators are fundamental components in all automated systems, from automotive to industrial robotics.

subjects.name: Microcontrollers & Applications
subjects.description: Study of microcontroller architecture, programming, and interfacing for mechatronic applications. Covers 8051, PIC, and ARM microcontrollers with emphasis on real-time control and peripheral interfacing.
learning_outcomes: Program microcontrollers for real-time control applications
Interface peripherals including ADC, PWM, and communication modules
Develop embedded firmware for mechatronic systems
Design microcontroller-based control circuits
subjects.industry_relevance: Microcontrollers are at the heart of all embedded mechatronic products, from automotive ECUs to industrial controllers.

subjects.name: PLC & SCADA
subjects.description: Programmable Logic Controllers and Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition systems for industrial automation. Covers ladder logic programming, HMI design, and industrial communication protocols.
learning_outcomes: Program PLCs using ladder logic and structured text
Design SCADA interfaces for industrial process monitoring
Implement industrial communication protocols (Modbus, Profibus)
Troubleshoot PLC-based control systems
subjects.industry_relevance: PLC and SCADA skills are essential for factory automation, process control, and Industry 4.0 implementations.

subjects.name: Hydraulics & Pneumatics
subjects.description: Study of fluid power systems including hydraulic and pneumatic circuits, pumps, valves, actuators, and control elements. Covers circuit design, system maintenance, and industrial applications.
learning_outcomes: Design hydraulic and pneumatic circuits for industrial applications
Select pumps, valves, and actuators for fluid power systems
Troubleshoot common faults in hydraulic and pneumatic systems
Analyze fluid power system performance and efficiency
subjects.industry_relevance: Hydraulic and pneumatic systems are widely used in manufacturing, construction, and automation industries.

subjects.name: Control Systems
subjects.description: Theory and application of control systems including transfer functions, state-space analysis, PID control, root locus, and frequency response methods. Covers both continuous and discrete control systems.
learning_outcomes: Model dynamic systems using transfer functions and state-space
Design PID and lead-lag compensators for desired performance
Analyze system stability using root locus and Bode plots
Implement digital control algorithms for real-time applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Control theory underpins all automated systems from industrial robots to autonomous vehicles.

subjects.name: Robotics
subjects.description: Fundamentals of robotics including robot kinematics, dynamics, trajectory planning, and control. Covers manipulator design, end effectors, sensors, and programming for industrial robot applications.
learning_outcomes: Derive forward and inverse kinematics for robotic manipulators
Plan collision-free trajectories for robot motion
Select end effectors and grippers for specific tasks
Program industrial robots for manufacturing applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Robotics is transforming manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare, creating high demand for robotics engineers.

subjects.name: CNC Technology
subjects.description: Computer Numerical Control technology including CNC machine tools, programming using G-code and M-code, tool path generation, and precision machining. Covers multi-axis machining and CAM integration.
learning_outcomes: Write CNC programs using G-code and M-code
Set up and operate CNC milling and turning machines
Generate tool paths using CAM software
Optimize machining parameters for precision and efficiency
subjects.industry_relevance: CNC technology is the backbone of precision manufacturing across aerospace, automotive, and tooling industries.

subjects.name: Industrial Automation
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of industrial automation systems including programmable logic controllers, distributed control systems, industrial networks, and robotic workcells. Covers Industry 4.0 and smart manufacturing concepts.
learning_outcomes: Design automated manufacturing workcells and production lines
Implement industrial communication networks for automation
Integrate robots, conveyors, and sensors in automated systems
Apply Industry 4.0 principles to smart factory design
subjects.industry_relevance: Industrial automation is driving the fourth industrial revolution, with massive demand for automation engineers globally.

subjects.name: Embedded Systems
subjects.description: Design and development of embedded systems for mechatronic applications including real-time operating systems, peripheral interfacing, and firmware development. Covers ARM Cortex-M architecture and RTOS concepts.
learning_outcomes: Design embedded systems using ARM microcontrollers
Implement real-time operating systems for time-critical tasks
Develop device drivers for sensors and actuators
Optimize embedded firmware for power and performance
subjects.industry_relevance: Embedded systems expertise is critical for IoT, automotive electronics, and consumer product development.

subjects.name: MEMS & Nanotechnology
subjects.description: Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems and nanotechnology principles including microfabrication, MEMS sensors and actuators, nano-materials, and their applications in mechatronics and biomedical systems.
learning_outcomes: Explain MEMS fabrication processes and materials
Design MEMS-based sensors and actuators
Analyze nano-scale material properties and behaviors
Evaluate applications of MEMS in automotive and biomedical fields
subjects.industry_relevance: MEMS technology powers accelerometers, gyroscopes, and pressure sensors used in automotive, consumer electronics, and healthcare.

subjects.name: Design of Mechatronic Systems
subjects.description: Systematic methodology for designing mechatronic products including requirement analysis, conceptual design, modeling, simulation, and prototyping. Covers design optimization and validation techniques.
learning_outcomes: Apply systematic design methodology to mechatronic products
Model and simulate mechatronic systems using software tools
Develop and test prototypes of mechatronic systems
Optimize designs for performance, cost, and reliability
subjects.industry_relevance: Systematic mechatronic design methodology is essential for developing competitive smart products across industries.

subjects.name: Intelligent Controllers
subjects.description: Advanced control strategies including fuzzy logic, neural networks, genetic algorithms, and adaptive control for complex mechatronic systems. Covers real-time implementation and performance optimization.
learning_outcomes: Design fuzzy logic controllers for non-linear systems
Implement neural network-based control strategies
Apply genetic algorithms for controller optimization
Develop adaptive controllers for time-varying systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Intelligent control techniques are essential for autonomous systems, advanced robotics, and smart manufacturing.

subjects.name: Machine Vision
subjects.description: Image processing and computer vision techniques for industrial inspection, object recognition, and robotic guidance. Covers camera systems, image filtering, feature extraction, and deep learning-based vision.
learning_outcomes: Process and analyze images using filtering and transformation techniques
Implement object detection and recognition algorithms
Design vision systems for industrial inspection applications
Integrate cameras and vision processing with robotic systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Machine vision is essential for quality inspection, autonomous navigation, and robotic guidance in Industry 4.0.

subjects.name: Electric Drives
subjects.description: Study of electric motor drives including DC, induction, synchronous, and brushless DC motors. Covers power electronic converters, speed control methods, and drive selection for industrial and automotive applications.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the characteristics of various electric motor types
Design power electronic converters for motor drives
Implement speed and torque control strategies for motors
Select appropriate drives for industrial and automotive applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Electric drives are fundamental to industrial automation, electric vehicles, and renewable energy systems.

subjects.name: Instrumentation & Measurements
subjects.description: Principles of measurement systems including transducers, signal conditioning, data acquisition, and error analysis. Covers measurement of physical parameters such as temperature, pressure, flow, and displacement.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate instruments for measuring physical parameters
Design signal conditioning circuits for sensor outputs
Analyze measurement errors and apply calibration techniques
Develop data acquisition systems for industrial monitoring
subjects.industry_relevance: Instrumentation is vital for quality control, process monitoring, and test and measurement across all engineering sectors.
